{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Identity Articles Undefined",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights scaffold (version 1.0.0-seed) contains no substantive definitions for its identity‑defining articles: I (Scope of Enterprise), II (Prohibited Activities), III (Core Identity), IV (Human Authority Reservation), V (Amendment Protocol), and VI (Interpretation Rules). Without these articles the organism has no constitutional perimeter, no prohibited activities, no core identity statement, and no defined reservation of human authority. Consequently, it is impossible to assess whether any current or future goal‑stream is constitutionally permissible. This is a foundational omission that renders all downstream constitutional analysis meaningless.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
        "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities",
        "BoR Article III — Core Identity",
        "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation",
        "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol",
        "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules"
      ],
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "conflict_with_goal": null,
      "note": "No recent goal‑stream is present; the gap is the absence of constitutional identity itself, which would instantly conflict with any goal that later emerges because no perimeter exists to validate it."
    }
  ]
}
